source_paper_title: Riemannian Anderson Mixing Methods for Minimizing C2 Functions on Riemannian Manifolds
context:
Page 10, Section 4 (Regularied RAM and Global Convergence Analysis); also reiterated Page 14, Section 5.1 (Details Concerning Numerical Implementations)

"The global convergence of Anderson mixing remains an problem, even in the Euclidean setting."
title: Global convergence of (unregularized) Anderson mixing / Riemannian Anderson Mixing
latex:
Given a complete Riemannian manifold \((\mathcal M,\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle)\) and a \(C^1\) vector field \(F\in \mathcal X(\mathcal M)\), define the fixed-point mapping \(g:\mathcal M\to\mathcal M\) by \(g(x)=\mathrm{Exp}_x(-F(x))\). Let \(R: T\mathcal M\to \mathcal M\) be a retraction with associated vector transport \(\mathcal T\). For a memory parameter \(m\in\mathbb N\), define the Riemannian Anderson Mixing (RAM) iterates \(\{x_k\}\subset \mathcal M\) by Algorithm 1 of the paper:
\[
\begin{aligned}
&r_k=-F(x_k)\in T_{x_k}\mathcal M,\qquad m_k=\min\{m,k\},\\
&\Gamma_k\in\arg\min_{\Gamma\in\mathbb R^{m_k}}\ \|r_k-R_k\Gamma\|,\\
&\Delta x_k^{(k)}=-X_k\Gamma_k+\beta_k\,(r_k-R_k\Gamma_k)\in T_{x_k}\mathcal M,\\
&x_{k+1}=R_{x_k}(\Delta x_k^{(k)}),
\end{aligned}
\]
where \(X_k\) and \(R_k\) are formed from transported differences of past iterates and residuals (as in Algorithm 1) so that their columns lie in \(T_{x_k}\mathcal M\), and \(\beta_k>0\) is a stepsize.

Problem: Determine verifiable conditions on \(F\), \(R\), \(\mathcal T\), \(m\), and the parameter sequence \(\{\beta_k\}\) under which the RAM iterates \(\{x_k\}\) are globally convergent, e.g. either \(\|F(x_k)\|\to 0\) or \(x_k\to x^*\) with \(F(x^*)=0\), from an arbitrary initialization \(x_0\in\mathcal M\), without introducing additional safeguarding steps (such as regularization or line-search).
